seoTitle: Mount Irvine vs Mount Kembla — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Irvine and Mount Kembla. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Irvine vs Mount Kembla: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Irvine and Mount Kembla.
heading: Mount Irvine vs Mount Kembla — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Irvine, New South Wales and Mount Kembla, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Irvine has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Mount Kembla (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Irvine or Mount Kembla safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Irvine has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Mount Kembla scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Irvine scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Kembla scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Irvine or Mount Kembla?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Irvine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
